author: potuz
content: yes we do vote for any block that appears

author: potuz
content: cause PTC is not about forkchoice, it's about whether the payload is timely or not

author: nflaig
content: meaning, any block you receive for the slot that is valid? and how do you pick if you receive 2 blocks from different branches with different proposer lookahead, do you pick the one with (1) more weight or (2) the "first seen" block?

author: nflaig
content:
there was a concern raised by NC doing this
> Can there be an attack where some proposer of the side chain just publish payload very early, then PTCs just attest timeliness and DA for that block, then there is no payload attestation for the canonical block that was published later into the slot?

author: nflaig
content: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1508479832596418692/1509400720028205167 which block should lodestar vote for here? the one on the other branch (from prysm) or the one on our canonical branch? if you use "first seen" as the criteria then it would be the non canonical one, which seems wrong in that case

author: potuz
content: We pick compatible with our head view if I remember correctly. At least that's what we meant to do

author: nflaig
content: what claude told me is that prysm picks the "first seen", which seems wrong, but might be wrong interpretation of the code

author: nflaig
content: <@&1417820113981145228> all clients besides prysm are not following the spec here if that's what we wanna do

author: nflaig
content: but if you say "compatible with head view" what does that mean? do you pick the shuffling from the head view? and why not from other branches?

author: potuz
content: Ah that actually may be true cause we moved to attest immediately

author: potuz
content: Perhaps we could send an equivocating PTC attestation in this case. Otherwise the attestation will always have to wait for the Payload

author: potuz
content: Or perhaps we can always wait in the case that we have seen multiple blocks

author: potuz
content: And attest immediately only when we have seen a single block

author: nflaig
content: wait but no, doesn't this mean in the case we had on the devnet, we should do 2 ptc votes on both branches?

author: potuz
content: I'm not sure. I feel in this case it's still better to wait and send only for your head view

author: potuz
content: And attest timely only if you're expecting a single Payload
